# Terraform MongoDB Atlas Endpoint Module

 [![Latest Release](https://img.shields.io/github/release/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-endpoint.svg?style=for-the-badge)](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-endpoint/releases/latest) [![Last Updated](https://img.shields.io/github/last-commit/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-endpoint.svg?style=for-the-badge)](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-endpoint/commits)


Terraform module for provisioning a MongoDB Atlas PrivateLink endpoint. Discovers an existing
Atlas network container to infer the cloud provider and region, then creates the corresponding
`mongodbatlas_privatelink_endpoint` resource. The Atlas project may be referenced by ID or name.


---

This project is part of our comprehensive approach towards DevOps Acceleration.

It's 100% Open Source and licensed under the [APACHE2](LICENSE).







We have [*lots of terraform modules*][terraform_modules] that are Open Source and we are trying to get them well-maintained!. Check them out!






## Introduction

This module automates the creation of a [MongoDB Atlas PrivateLink endpoint](https://www.mongodb.com/docs/atlas/security-private-endpoint/)
by leveraging an existing Atlas network container. It resolves the target project either by a
supplied project ID or by performing a name-based lookup, and derives the cloud provider and
region directly from the network container â€” removing the need to hard-code those values.

### Key features

- Supports project lookup by **ID** or by **name** (mutually exclusive)
- Automatically infers **cloud provider** (`AWS`, `AZURE`, `GCP`) and **region** from the Atlas
  network container
- Emits the PrivateLink **ID**, **service name**, and **status** as outputs for downstream use
  (e.g., creating the cloud-side VPC/private endpoint)
- Follows CloudOps Works tagging and naming conventions via the `cloudopsworks/tags/local` module

### Prerequisites

- Terraform `>= 1.7`
- MongoDB Atlas provider `~> 2.1`
- An existing MongoDB Atlas network container (created by a companion module such as
  `terraform-module-mongoatlas-container`)
- Atlas API credentials configured via AWS Secrets Manager (STS role ARN + secret name)

## Usage


**IMPORTANT:** The `master` branch is used in `source` just as an example. In your code, do not pin to `master` because there may be breaking changes between releases.
Instead pin to the release tag (e.g. `?ref=vX.Y.Z`) of one of our [latest releases](https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-endpoint/releases).


The module is designed to be consumed through **Terragrunt**. A typical directory layout
places this configuration alongside a companion `atlas-network-container` module and an
`atlas-project` module.

**`terragrunt.hcl`**

```hcl
locals {
  local_vars  = yamldecode(file("./inputs.yaml"))
  spoke_vars  = yamldecode(file(find_in_parent_folders("spoke-inputs.yaml")))
  region_vars = yamldecode(file(find_in_parent_folders("region-inputs.yaml")))
  env_vars    = yamldecode(file(find_in_parent_folders("env-inputs.yaml")))
  global_vars = yamldecode(file(find_in_parent_folders("global-inputs.yaml")))

  local_tags  = jsondecode(file("./local-tags.json"))
  spoke_tags  = jsondecode(file(find_in_parent_folders("spoke-tags.json")))
  region_tags = jsondecode(file(find_in_parent_folders("region-tags.json")))
  env_tags    = jsondecode(file(find_in_parent_folders("env-tags.json")))
  global_tags = jsondecode(file(find_in_parent_folders("global-tags.json")))

  tags = merge(
    local.global_tags,
    local.env_tags,
    local.region_tags,
    local.spoke_tags,
    local.local_tags
  )
}

include "root" {
  path = find_in_parent_folders("root.hcl")
}

# Generates the MongoDB Atlas provider block at plan/apply time using credentials
# stored in AWS Secrets Manager (STS-based authentication).
generate "provider-mongoatlas" {
  path      = "provider-mongoatlas.g.tf"
  if_exists = "overwrite_terragrunt"
  contents  = <<EOF
provider "mongodbatlas" {
  assume_role {
    role_arn = "${local.global_vars.mongodb_atlas.secrets.sts_role_arn}"
  }
  secret_name = "${local.global_vars.mongodb_atlas.secrets.name}"
  region      = "${local.global_vars.mongodb_atlas.secrets.region}"
}
EOF
}

dependency "atlascontainer" {
  config_path = "../atlas-network-container"

  mock_outputs_allowed_terraform_commands = ["validate", "destroy"]
  mock_outputs = {
    network_container_id = "5e2211c17a3e5a48f5497de3"
  }
}

dependency "project" {
  config_path = "../atlas-project"

  mock_outputs_allowed_terraform_commands = ["validate", "destroy"]
  mock_outputs = {
    project_id = "5e1b39a5e4f9b74b7d8b456c"
  }
}

terraform {
  source = "git::https://github.com/cloudopsworks/terraform-module-mongoatlas-endpoint.git?ref=v1.0.0"
}

inputs = {
  is_hub    = false
  org       = local.env_vars.org
  spoke_def = local.spoke_vars.spoke

  # Atlas network container supplying the provider + region for the endpoint
  atlas_container_id = dependency.atlascontainer.outputs.network_container_id

  # Provide EITHER project_id OR project_name â€” not both
  project_id = dependency.project.outputs.project_id
  # project_name = local.local_vars.project_name  # alternative: lookup by name

  extra_tags = local.tags
}
```

**`inputs.yaml`** (local overrides when not using a project dependency)

```yaml
# atlas_container_id is supplied via the atlascontainer dependency above.
# Uncomment project_name below if not using a project dependency.
# project_name: "my-atlas-project"
```

### Module variables

| Variable | Type | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `org` | `object` | Yes | Organisation/environment metadata used for tagging and naming |
| `org.organization_name` | `string` | Yes | Full organisation name |
| `org.organization_unit` | `string` | Yes | Business/team unit abbreviation |
| `org.environment_type` | `string` | Yes | Environment type (e.g. `production`, `staging`) |
| `org.environment_name` | `string` | Yes | Short environment name (e.g. `prod`, `stg`) |
| `atlas_container_id` | `string` | Yes | ID of the existing MongoDB Atlas network container |
| `project_id` | `string` | No | MongoDB Atlas project ID. Mutually exclusive with `project_name`. Default: `""` |
| `project_name` | `string` | No | MongoDB Atlas project name for ID lookup. Mutually exclusive with `project_id`. Default: `""` |
| `is_hub` | `bool` | No | Whether this is a hub (vs spoke) configuration. Default: `false` |
| `spoke_def` | `string` | No | Spoke identifier suffix used in resource naming. Default: `"001"` |
| `extra_tags` | `map(string)` | No | Additional tags merged with the standard tag set. Default: `{}` |

### Outputs

| Output | Description |
|---|---|
| `endpoint_privatelink_id` | The PrivateLink ID returned by MongoDB Atlas (used to create the cloud-side endpoint) |
| `endpoint_privatelink_service_name` | The endpoint service name (AWS VPC endpoint service or Azure private link service) |
| `endpoint_status` | Current status of the PrivateLink endpoint (`INITIATING`, `AVAILABLE`, `WAITING_FOR_USER`, `FAILED`, `DELETING`) |

## Quick Start

1. **Initialise the module** in your implementation repository (MongoDB Atlas boilerplate):
   ```sh
   make init/mongodb
   ```

2. **Create your Terragrunt layout**:
   ```
   envs/
     prod/
       us-east-1/
         spoke-001/
           atlas-network-container/   # prerequisite module
           atlas-project/             # prerequisite module
           atlas-endpoint/
             terragrunt.hcl           # based on .boilerplate/terragrunt.hcl
             inputs.yaml
             local-tags.json
   ```

3. **Copy and customise** `.boilerplate/terragrunt.hcl` into your `atlas-endpoint/` directory.
   Update the `dependency` paths and the `terraform.source` ref to point at the desired release tag.

4. **Validate and apply**:
   ```sh
   make fmt
   make lint
   terragrunt plan
   terragrunt apply
   ```

5. **Use the outputs** in the cloud-side endpoint module (e.g., AWS VPC Endpoint):
   - `endpoint_privatelink_id` â†’ pass to your AWS/Azure/GCP endpoint resource as the service ID
   - `endpoint_privatelink_service_name` â†’ pass as the VPC endpoint service name


## Examples

### Example: AWS PrivateLink endpoint (project referenced by ID)

```hcl
inputs = {
  is_hub    = false
  org = {
    organization_name = "Acme Corp"
    organization_unit = "platform"
    environment_type  = "production"
    environment_name  = "prod"
  }
  spoke_def = "001"

  atlas_container_id = dependency.atlascontainer.outputs.network_container_id
  project_id         = dependency.project.outputs.project_id

  extra_tags = { "CostCenter" = "12345" }
}
```

### Example: Lookup project by name (no project dependency)

```hcl
inputs = {
  is_hub    = false
  org = {
    organization_name = "Acme Corp"
    organization_unit = "platform"
    environment_type  = "staging"
    environment_name  = "stg"
  }
  spoke_def = "001"

  atlas_container_id = dependency.atlascontainer.outputs.network_container_id
  project_name       = "acme-staging-project"

  extra_tags = {}
}
```



## Makefile Targets
```
Available targets:

  help                                Help screen
  help/all                            Display help for all targets
  help/short                          This help short screen
  init/%                              Initialize the project for a specific cloud provider: %S
  lint                                Lint terraform/opentofu code
  tag                                 Tag the current version

```

## Inputs

| Name | Description | Type | Default | Required |
|------|-------------|------|---------|:--------:|
| <a name="input_atlas_container_id"></a> [atlas\_container\_id](#input\_atlas\_container\_id) | (Required) The ID of the MongoDB Atlas network container. Used to determine the cloud provider and region for the PrivateLink endpoint. | `string` | n/a | yes |
| <a name="input_extra_tags"></a> [extra\_tags](#input\_extra\_tags) | Extra tags to add to the resources | `map(string)` | `{}` | no |
| <a name="input_is_hub"></a> [is\_hub](#input\_is\_hub) | Is this a hub or spoke configuration? | `bool` | `false` | no |
| <a name="input_org"></a> [org](#input\_org) | Organization details | <pre>object({<br/>    organization_name = string<br/>    organization_unit = string<br/>    environment_type  = string<br/>    environment_name  = string<br/>  })</pre> | n/a | yes |
| <a name="input_project_id"></a> [project\_id](#input\_project\_id) | (Optional) The ID of the MongoDB Atlas project. Mutually exclusive with project\_name; provide one or the other. | `string` | `""` | no |
| <a name="input_project_name"></a> [project\_name](#input\_project\_name) | (Optional) The name of the MongoDB Atlas project. Used to resolve the project\_id when project\_id is not provided. Mutually exclusive with project\_id. | `string` | `""` | no |
| <a name="input_spoke_def"></a> [spoke\_def](#input\_spoke\_def) | Spoke ID Number, must be a 3 digit number | `string` | `"001"` | no |

## Outputs

| Name | Description |
|------|-------------|
| <a name="output_endpoint_privatelink_id"></a> [endpoint\_privatelink\_id](#output\_endpoint\_privatelink\_id) | n/a |
| <a name="output_endpoint_privatelink_service_name"></a> [endpoint\_privatelink\_service\_name](#output\_endpoint\_privatelink\_service\_name) | n/a |
| <a name="output_endpoint_status"></a> [endpoint\_status](#output\_endpoint\_status) | n/a |
